name: ST2110 Out TOP
description: Support for Blackmagic, AJA and Deltacast ST2110 devices. To obtain the SDPs generated for the output essences, use an [[Info DAT]] and choose which SDP to obtain. See also [[ST2110 In TOP]], [[ST2110 Device CHOP]].

parameters.name: Active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Controls if this node is sending out data over the ST2110 device.

parameters.name: ST2110 Device CHOP
parameters.label: ST2110 Device CHOP
parameters.description: Since a NIC has one set of settings such as DHCP/IP settings, that is controlled for each device using the ST2110 Device CHOP. This node will use that device to discover available output channels, and use the settings configured in that node for it's operation.

parameters.name: Device
parameters.label: Device
parameters.description: Will be populated when the ST2110 Device CHOP is active and configured properly. Select which output channel on the device to use.

parameters.name: Video Destination IP
parameters.label: Video Destination IP
parameters.description: The multicast IP address that the video essence should be sent on.

parameters.name: Video Source Port
parameters.label: Video Source Port
parameters.description: The local network port number that the video essence should be sent from. Some devices will auto-select this port.

parameters.name: Video Destination Port
parameters.label: Video Destination Port
parameters.description: The destination network port number that the video essence should be sent to.

parameters.name: Video Payload Id
parameters.label: Video Payload Id
parameters.description: The Payload Id number to assign to the RTP packets for the video essence. Usually 96 for video.

parameters.name: Signal Format
parameters.label: Signal Format
parameters.description: The Video Signal Format to send the video as.

parameters.name: Output Pixel Format
parameters.label: Output Pixel Format
parameters.description: The Pixel Format to send data over the wire as.

parameters.name: Timecode Object/CHOP/DAT
parameters.label: Timecode Object/CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: For devices that support timecode, this can point to a [[Timecode]] source to send over the ancillary essence.

parameters.name: Memory Mode
parameters.label: Memory Mode
parameters.description: Controls how the video data is copied to the output device.

parameters.name: Audio CHOP
parameters.label: Audio CHOP
parameters.description: A timesliced audio CHOP that is either 44110 or 48000 Hz, but not all devices support both of those sample rates. This will be sent out over the audio essence.

parameters.name: Audio Buffer Length
parameters.label: Audio Buffer Length
parameters.description: How long of an audio buffer queue to hold. This helps avoid audio pops in the event video generation stutters.

parameters.name: Audio Dest IP
parameters.label: Audio Dest IP
parameters.description: The multicast IP address that the audio essence should be sent on.

parameters.name: Audio Source Port
parameters.label: Audio Source Port
parameters.description: The local network port number that the audio essence should be sent from. Some devices will auto-select this port.

parameters.name: Audio Destination Port
parameters.label: Audio Destination Port
parameters.description: The destination network port number that the audio essence should be sent to.

parameters.name: Audio Payload Id
parameters.label: Audio Payload Id
parameters.description: The Payload Id number to assign to the RTP packets for the audio essence. Usually 97 for audio.

parameters.name: Enable SPS
parameters.label: Enable SPS
parameters.description: Controls if the SPS stream is active or not.

parameters.name: SPS Video Destination IP
parameters.label: SPS Video Destination IP
parameters.description: The multicast IP address that the video SPS essence should be sent on.

parameters.name: SPS Video Source Port
parameters.label: SPS Video Source Port
parameters.description: The local network port number that the video SPS essence should be sent from. Some devices will auto-select this port.

parameters.name: SPS Video Destination Port
parameters.label: SPS Video Destination Port
parameters.description: The destination network port number that the video SPS essence should be sent to.

parameters.name: SPS Video Payload Id
parameters.label: SPS Video Payload Id
parameters.description: The Payload Id number to assign to the RTP packets for the SPS video essence. Usually 96 for video.

parameters.name: SPS Audio Dest IP
parameters.label: SPS Audio Dest IP
parameters.description: The multicast IP address that the audio SPS essence should be sent on.

parameters.name: SPS Audio Source Port
parameters.label: SPS Audio Source Port
parameters.description: The local network port number that the audio SPS essence should be sent from. Some devices will auto-select this port.

parameters.name: SPS Audio Destination Port
parameters.label: SPS Audio Destination Port
parameters.description: The destination network port number that the audio SPS essence should be sent to.

parameters.name: SPS Audio Payload Id
parameters.label: SPS Audio Payload Id
parameters.description: The Payload Id number to assign to the RTP packets for the audio SPS essence. Usually 97 for audio.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the live ST2110_Out_TOP wiki page
